summary: Dependency confusion package exfiltrates host reconnaissance via HTTP POST
details: The package cb.js requires child_process, executes reconnaissance commands including `whoami` and `hostname`, and transmits the collected output via an `http.request` POST call. This pattern — spawning local recon commands and POSTing their output to a remote endpoint — is a classic host reconnaissance beacon with no legitimate purpose in a design-system web library. The package name and 999.0.0 version are consistent with a dependency-confusion attack targeting an internal `@design-system-coopeuch/web` scope, where the attacker publishes a high-version public package so build systems resolving against the public registry pull the malicious version. Installing or loading this package leaks host identity (username, hostname) to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
